content:
I just found out in the Linux kernel that for any source file kernel/foo.c you can run "make kernel/foo.i" to get the preprocessed source

I just heard of this feature today. Can we maybe add something like this to the rust part, expanding all (proc)macros, to make rust modules easier to debug?
Sounds good, I can take a look -- could you please open an issue to not forget about it?

Rustc has the unstable -Zunpretty=expanded flag for this. You will probably want to run the output through rustfmt though (the default formatting is based on a very old style and has a lot of rightward drift. expect lines >100 chars for anything using format_args!()) It can also get pretty long if you use many modules as they all get inlined.
Is rustfmt a build dependency? IIRC nix currently does not have it in scope. Would like to detect if it’s there, and only used it if found, to make debugging failed build in nix (and similar) easier

Finn Behrens said:

Is rustfmt a build dependency? IIRC nix currently does not have it in scope. Would like to detect if it’s there, and only used it if found, to make debugging failed build in nix (and similar) easier

Looked at it, currently my nix files do have rustfmt to build the kernel, would be nice if it would be optional, no-one will look at the bindgen file, if it's just a build assets, as it gets cleaned after build. (of course only for builds, for dev it's different)
